title: Pick Location on Map
excerpt: We do quite a bit of work in rural Canada where addresses are not in a format which Google will recognize or we do not have an address for a location.  To help us specify locations it would be awesome of the location col…

posts.author: darren (Darren Wiebe)
posts.cookedHtml:
We do quite a bit of work in rural Canada where addresses are not in a format which Google will recognize or we do not have an address for a location. To help us specify locations it would be awesome of the location column had a map popup we could use to pin the location.

Here’s what I envision:

  1. Click on map indicator to bring up a map. It should be centered on your current location based on device GPS if on mobile or your location based on the browser.
  2. Either click on the map to select the current location or navigate with the map to the correct location before selecting a location.

This would make the location column much more useful for us.

Somewhat related:

Allow Use of GPS Coordinates in Location Field Feature requests
The mapping feature (location) in Monday is one the MAIN reasons we switched from what we were using (after 10 years) and it is great to get real time map updates on tickets, schedules, etc. It would be VERY helpful if we could use GPS coordinates, as we have some locations and map areas that we’d love to pin point, but they are not tied to physical addresses that we can input in the location field. I tried using some GPS cords as a test, but I do not get any pins to display when in map view, …
Precision of Location Column Platform discussions
I have a bunch of items that have locations that are tens of metres apart. Their “address” is a geographic coordinate in latitude and longitude in decimal degrees. However, when these items plot on the Map view, they all bunch up in the same location, 50 - 100m away from where I would expect it to be. What I’m seeing: [image] Where these points should be plotting: [image] Can you confirm the precision of the link between my Location pulse and Google Maps? I know that Google Maps itself…

posts.author: bob1 (bob)
posts.cookedHtml:
I second this request.

In our case, we have a list of future buildings that we plan to build. The issue is that many of those buildings don’t have an address yet because they’re located on raw undevelopped land.

I can go into Google Maps, pinpoint the location, copy the geocoordinates and paste them into my location column in Monday. It works fine, but being able to do the whole process directly into Monday would be a nice quality of life improvement.

posts.author: bob1 (bob)
posts.cookedHtml:
After further testing, copy/pasting geocoordinates doesn’t work that well either. For some reason the markers are “sticky”. If you pinpoint a specific location near an existing building, it will move your marker onto that building instead of displaying it at the exact geocoordinates that you entered. Very annoying behaviour and no workaround for that.

I have 4 buildings with different geocoordinates but they all appear as one marker and they’re located onto a nearby existing building instead of their actual location because of that sticky behaviour.

posts.author: Jappa (Jasper Schulten)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have posted the same issue. For now they won’t do anything with it.

But if you use the Location column in the app to navigate or to display it in Google Maps (for instance), it shows the correct location. Just in Monday Maps it shows a wrong location (nearest address).

posts.author: j.zirklewski (Jörg)
posts.cookedHtml:
A Field for Longitude and Latitude would be nice.
When a photo is uploaded in updates it would be very helpful if the location information of this photo could be transferred into a location field too.

posts.author: MCautreels (Maarten Cautreels - Tryve)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone,

I know your original post is from a while back but we’ve recently added a new feature to our monday Marketplace app Location Toolkit: Map Picker!

This adds an item view which allows you to select a location on a map in the first place but it also shows you the selected/configured location on a map just like this:

Screenshot 2024-06-25 at 22.56.591920×1100 224 KB

I hope this is useful and do let us know if you have any feedback or feature requests!

Best,

Maarten
